title: Module 1
content:
Safety domain terminologies, Hazard, Risk, Mishap, Incident, Hazard
triangle, Risk Assessment & Control,  Qualitative Perspective, Qualitative
Perspective, Safety Engineering & Accident causing mechanisms, Safety
quantification, Preliminary Hazard List, Preliminary Hazard Analysis,
Hazard and operability study (HAZOP), Failure Modes and Effects Analysis
(FMEA)- Identification of Failure Modes, Application of Hazard
Identification Techniques, Fault Tree Analysis (FTA)- Construction, Gate by
Gate method, Cut-set method, Importance measures.

title: Module 2
content:
Event Tree Analysis (ETA), Bowtie Tool, Common Cause Cut Sets, Cut-sets
for Accident Scenarios, Identification of Safety Barriers, Risk Assessment,
Consequence Assessment, Energy Control Model and Hazard Control
Hierarchy, Safety Function Deployment, Ranking of Design Solutions: AHP
approach, Quantification of Basic Events for Non-repairable Components,
Hazard Rate, Exponential Distribution, Weibull Distribution.

title: Module 3
content:
Reliability engineering, Basic concepts – Evaluation of network Reliability /
Unreliability – Series systems, Parallel systems, Series - Parallel systems,
partially redundant systems – Types of redundancies - Evaluation of network
Reliability / Unreliability using conditional probability method. Time
Dependent Probability Basic concepts Failure, Fault, Error, Failure mode,
Failure classification, Markovian models, Common cause and common
mode failures, – Reliability functions f(t), F(t), R(t), h(t) – Relationship
between these functions .

title: Module 4
content:
Baths tubs curve – Exponential failure density and distribution functions -
Expected value and standard deviation of Exponential distribution –
Measures of reliability – MTTF, MTTR, MTBF – Evaluation of network
reliability / Unreliability of simple Series, Parallel, Series-Parallel systems -
Partially redundant systems - Evaluation of reliability measure – MTTF for
series and parallel systems – Examples. Monte Carlo simulation, Application
to uncertainty analysis, Simple integration, Trivial integration.
